body.question_1: How would you rate the university's communication with parents regarding your child's academic progress and welfare?
body.answer_1: Communication from the School of Engineering to parents regarding student progress is satisfactory and fulfills the minimum requirements, but falls short of what a genuinely family-supportive institution should provide. The parent portal provides access to basic grade and attendance data, but the interface is somewhat outdated and not particularly intuitive for parents who are not technology-confident. Proactive outreach — for example, early alerts when a student's performance or attendance begins to decline — is currently limited and reactive. More frequent, personalized, and timely communication from the Academic Affairs Office would significantly strengthen the partnership between parents and the institution in supporting student success.

body.question_2: How satisfied are you with the quality of education your child is receiving at this university?
body.answer_2: Teaching quality in my Mechanical Engineering program at the School of Engineering has been reasonable overall, though noticeably inconsistent across different modules this semester. Several lecturers deliver material with clarity and genuine enthusiasm, making those sessions highly productive and intellectually stimulating for the class. However, a few courses suffer from disorganized delivery, limited interaction, and minimal feedback on submitted work, which hampers the learning experience considerably. I would recommend the school to standardize teaching quality benchmarks and conduct regular peer evaluations to ensure a more consistent and high-quality educational experience for all students across every module and year level.

body.question_3: How confident are you in the university's ability to prepare your child for future career opportunities?
body.answer_3: Career preparation support from the Career Center at the School of Engineering has been insufficient and has left many students — including myself — feeling ill-equipped for the competitive graduate employment market. Career workshops are infrequent, poorly promoted, and often scheduled at times that conflict with lecture commitments, reducing student participation. The internship database is outdated and contains many positions that are no longer active, and support for students during internship placements is minimal to nonexistent. I suggest the university to substantially reinvest in the Career Center — expanding its employer network, modernizing its tools, and increasing the frequency and quality of career development programming across all academic programs and year levels.

body.question_4: How would you assess the campus safety measures and student welfare programs in place at this institution?
body.answer_4: Campus safety standards at the School of Engineering are adequate, providing an adequate baseline of physical security and student welfare programming while leaving meaningful room for improvement in several critical areas. Core security measures — including campus access controls, security patrols, and emergency contact systems — are functional and reasonably communicated. However, mental health and counseling resources are stretched and insufficiently staffed relative to the student population, creating unacceptable wait times during periods of peak demand. I would strongly encourage the Student Affairs Office to expand counseling capacity, improve lighting and surveillance in underserved campus areas, and develop a more proactive student welfare outreach program for at-risk individuals.

body.question_5: How transparent and straightforward have you found the university's financial policies, fee management, and scholarship communication?
body.answer_5: The financial transparency and management provided by the Finance Department at the School of Engineering has been commendable, and I genuinely praise the clarity with which tuition fees, scholarship entitlements, and payment deadlines are communicated to both students and their families. Fee breakdowns are detailed, accurate, and issued well in advance of payment deadlines, allowing adequate financial planning time. The installment payment option is a genuinely thoughtful and practical provision that reduces financial stress without compromising the institution's financial management requirements. Scholarship disbursements have been timely and confirmed through official notifications, reinforcing trust in the university's commitment to honoring its financial obligations to students and families.

body.question_6: How effective do you find the parent portal and digital communication systems provided by the university?
body.answer_6: The parent portal provided by the School of Engineering, supported by the IT Support Department, is remarkable in its design, functionality, and reliability, and has become an invaluable tool for staying connected to my child's academic journey throughout the year. The interface is intuitive and well-organized, presenting grade information, attendance records, fee statements, and university announcements in a clear and accessible format that requires no technical expertise. The system is reliably available, with minimal downtime, and notifications are pushed promptly for important academic events and institutional announcements. I commend the university's investment in digital parent engagement tools, which I believe genuinely strengthen the home-university partnership and support better student outcomes.

body.question_7: How satisfied are you with the academic counseling and advising support your child receives?
body.answer_7: Academic counseling and advising services at the School of Engineering, coordinated by the Academic Affairs Office, have been excellent in supporting my child's academic planning and decision-making throughout the year. Assigned academic advisors are knowledgeable, approachable, and proactively schedule meetings at key decision points in the academic calendar — such as course selection, internship planning, and graduation preparation. The quality and consistency of advice my child has received has given our family confidence that their academic trajectory is being thoughtfully guided by experienced professionals who understand both the program requirements and the student's individual goals. This service is clearly a strength of the institution.

body.question_8: How well does the university address students' mental health and personal well-being needs?
body.answer_8: Student support services at the School of Engineering, managed by the Student Affairs Office, are concerning and fall significantly short of what students need to manage the academic, personal, and financial challenges of university life. Mental health support in particular is severely limited — appointment wait times can exceed three weeks, which is unacceptable for students experiencing acute stress. Financial hardship assistance processes are slow, bureaucratic, and poorly communicated, leaving vulnerable students without timely help during critical periods. The university must urgently expand its counseling capacity, simplify hardship application procedures, and raise awareness of available services through more proactive and visible outreach to the student community.

body.question_9: How would you evaluate the quality and suitability of the university's physical facilities and campus environment?
body.answer_9: Campus facilities at the School of Engineering, maintained by the Facilities Management, are generally reasonable and adequate for day-to-day academic activities, though several areas require attention and investment to meet growing needs. Core academic spaces — including lecture halls and laboratories — are functional and reasonably maintained, though some equipment is beginning to show age and inconsistency in performance. Student study and relaxation spaces are insufficient during peak periods, and the condition of older campus buildings varies considerably. A systematic facilities audit followed by a prioritized improvement plan would help address longstanding issues and ensure the campus environment supports the academic ambitions of all students more effectively.

body.question_10: What improvements would you suggest to enhance the university's support for both students and their families?
body.answer_10: My overall assessment of the School of Engineering as a parent is that it offers a decent educational experience with genuine strengths that are unfortunately balanced by structural weaknesses that need sustained attention. The academic programs are broadly well-designed, and there are individual faculty members and support staff who clearly go above and beyond for students. However, system-level inconsistencies in service quality, communication, and resource provision create an uneven experience that depends too heavily on which individual staff members a student encounters. I encourage the institution to invest in building reliable systems and consistent standards that ensure every student — regardless of program or year — receives the same high-quality support and educational experience.
